data.title: VOW: Sales and profit declined amid market and operational challenges; cautious outlook for 2026
data.description:
Deliveries and sales revenue declined year-over-year, with operating profit pressured by US production changes and market headwinds. The outlook for 2026 is cautious, with stable deliveries and modest revenue growth expected amid ongoing geopolitical and industry risks.

Original document: Volkswagen AG [VOW] Interim report — Apr. 30 2026

data.title: Sweden's Q-linea Q1 net sales fall, consumables sales rise 60%
data.description:
Overview

  • Sweden infection diagnostics firm's Q1 net sales fell yr/yr to SEK 2 mln, while consumables sales rose 60% to SEK 1.4 mln

  • Q1 adjusted EPS loss narrowed from prior year

  • Company completed restructuring, delivering annual savings of about SEK 16 mln

Outlook

  • Q-Linea expects strong visibility for U.S. instrument placements in H2 2026 as contracting concludes

  • Company anticipates increased U.S. consumables usage in Q2 2026 as more instruments come online

  • Q-Linea plans to launch a dedicated Isolate Kit for RUO applications in Q3 2026

Result Drivers

  • CONSUMABLES GROWTH - Co said consumables sales rose as installed base grew and March saw record global shipments, with Italy and the Gulf region as main contributors

  • COST REDUCTION - Co said transition to fully internal production of consumables at start of year led to significant reduction in cost of goods sold

  • RESTRUCTURING SAVINGS - Co said completed restructuring delivered annual run-rate savings of over SEK 16 mln, with lower operating expenses visible in Q1

Company press release:

data.title: Germany's Takkt Q1 sales fall on weak demand, contract exit
data.description:
Overview

  • Germany business equipment distributor's Q1 sales fell 10.3% yr/yr, organic sales down 6.7%

  • Adjusted EBITDA margin dropped to 2.4% from 4.9% due to lower sales and margin pressure

  • Company cites weak demand, currency effects, and contract business exit as main factors in results

Outlook

  • Takkt expects 2026 organic sales development between minus 7% and plus 3%

  • Company sees 2026 adjusted EBITDA margin in the range of 2% to 5%

  • Takkt expects positive free cash flow in 2026 unless EBITDA margin is at lower end of range

Result Drivers

  • WEAK CUSTOMER DEMAND - Takkt said economic and geopolitical uncertainty led customers to hold back on major project investments, especially in Europe

  • CURRENCY EFFECTS - Weaker US dollar accounted for just over one-third of the year-over-year sales decline

  • FOODSERVICES CONTRACT EXIT - Discontinuation of bid contract business in Foodservices weighed on organic growth and sales

data.title: DCC Takeover Offer Value Unlikely to Exceed 10% Premium — Market Talk
data.description: Marketing and support-services provider DCC is likely to agree to a takeover offer but the price is unlikely to be more than 10% above its current share value, RBC Capital Markets's Andrew Brooke writes. The London-listed company said Wednesday that it received an indicative cash takeover offer from Energy Capital Partners and Kohlberg Kravis Roberts & Co. that it was reviewing, but didn't disclose any price. "We are not overly surprised about the approach, given its move to a pure-play and inexpensive headline valuation," Brooke says, adding that there's a good chance that a deal will happen. Shares closed Wednesday at 58.80 pence, having hit a high of 62.65 pence during the session. (ian.walker@wsj.com)

data.title: Danske Bank Q1 in line, announces extraordinary dividend
data.description:
Danske Bank , Denmark's biggest lender, on Thursday reported first-quarter net profit in line with expectations, and announced an extraordinary dividend of 6.14 Danish crowns ($0.9582) per share as it maintained its full-year outlook.

Danske Bank said net profit for the January to March period eased to 5.69 billion Danish crowns from 5.76 billion a year earlier, in line with the 5.67 billion expected on average by 14 analysts in a poll provided by the bank.

Going forward, Danske Bank will pay out between 60% and 70% of its net profit as regular annual dividend, up from a previous target of paying between 40% and 60%, it said in a separate statement.

($1 = 6.4079 Danish crowns)

data.title: ING launches 1 billion euro buyback as profit beats expectations
data.description:
ING Groep on Thursday launched a 1 billion euro ($1.2 billion) share buyback as it beat quarterly profit expectations, boosted by a strong performance across the board with costs lower than anticipated.

The bank's first-quarter profit stood at 1.56 billion euros compared with the 1.43 billion euros forecast by analysts polled by the lender and around 100 million euros higher than last year's result.

"Total income has risen 3% (...) supported by strong commercial net interest income and a 13% year-on-year increase in fee income," the lender's CEO Steven van Rijswijk said in a statement.

ING said the double-digit increase in fee income was partly driven by higher customer trading activity, boosting its push to lift net fee and commission income as lower interest rates weigh on lending revenues.

On Wednesday, Swiss bank UBS reported a more than 27% jump in investment banking revenue, in a sign European banks are following those on Wall Street to benefit from choppy market conditions.

The company confirmed its outlook for 2026 and 2027.

($1 = 0.8578 euros)

data.title: DANSKE: Q1 2026 net profit reached DKK 5.7bn, with resilient credit quality and strong capital ratios
data.description:
Net profit for Q1 2026 was DKK 5.7 billion, with strong customer activity and robust credit quality, but lower trading and insurance income due to market volatility. The cost/income ratio was 45.8%, and return on equity 13.1%. Revised 2028 targets include a return on equity above 14.5%.

Original document: Danske Bank A/S [DANSKE] Interim report — May. 1 2026

data.title: Duration of Mideast Conflict Likely Key Driver of U.S. Rates Outlook — Market Talk
data.description: The U.S. interest rates outlook for the rest of the year will increasingly be determined by the duration of the Middle East conflict, Fidelity International's Max Stainton says in a note. Fidelity's base case continues to shave dovish versus market pricing, expecting incoming Fed Chair Warsh, and the broader committee will want to lean against the growth damage from the energy shock. However, with the risks of the Strait of Hormuz staying closed for longer rising, there are clear risks that the energy price shock starts broadening out into a larger inflation shock across the whole of the economy, the senior global macro strategist says. "We still expect one cut this year, but the risks are clearly skewed to 'no action' for the rest of the year." (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: NMDC Group posts $1.79bln revenues in Q1
data.description:
Staff Writer

ABU DHABI - NMDC Group, a global leader in engineering, procurement, construction and marine dredging, today announced its financial results for the first quarter of 2026, reflecting resilient operational performance across its different vertical despite challenges amid evolving regional conditions.

The UAE has continued to demonstrate stability and continuity, supported by a coordinated and proactive government response that ensured the uninterrupted functioning of critical infrastructure and sustained business confidence across key sectors.

NMDC Group maintained consistent progress across its projects, supported by disciplined execution and strong operational coordination.

Revenues reached AED6.6 billion in Q1 2026, representing a 7 percent year-on-year increase, driven by steady backlog conversion.

Net profit stood at AED387 million, reflecting a measured and prudent approach in a more complex operating environment.

The Group’s backlog stood at AED55.4 billion, providing strong visibility on future revenues, while its pipeline reached AED92 billion across its verticals, NMDC Energy, NMDC Dredging & Marine, and NMDC Infra, highlighting the breadth of opportunities across its core sectors.

During the quarter, NMDC Group continued to build momentum within its Infrastructure vertical, NMDC Infra, focusing on essential infrastructure projects aligned with national priorities.

The acquisition of Lantania Aguas strengthens the Group’s position in the global water sector, while the NMDC Group and CCC joint venture enhances its capabilities in onshore oil and gas EPC, expanding its offering across the energy value chain.

Awarded projects during the period reached AED1.8 billion, with AED1.5 billion secured by Dredging & Marine and AED0.3 billion by Infra, reflecting sustained demand across the Group’s key business areas.

The Group maintained a net cash position of AED5.2 billion, reinforcing its financial strength and ability to navigate market conditions while continuing to invest in strategic growth areas.

Mohamed Thani Al Rumaithi, Chairman of the Board of Directors, NMDC Group, said, “The UAE continues to demonstrate that stability, clear policy direction and long-term planning are powerful enablers of economic progress. For NMDC Group, this provides the confidence to invest in capabilities, partnerships and sectors that will support the UAE’s continued economic diversification. Our first quarter performance reflects our unmatched business model, the strength of our balance sheet and our continued focus on creating sustainable long-term value for shareholders and stakeholders alike.”

Dr. Yasser Zaghloul, Group Chief Executive Officer of NMDC Group, stated, “Our first quarter results is a testament of our operational resilience across our portfolio, reflecting the strength of our operating model and the UAE’s stable and well-regulated economic infrastructure environment. We delivered steady revenue growth, maintained profitability and strengthened our backlog, while continuing to expand into strategic growth areas including infrastructure, water and onshore energy services. We remain well positioned to capture new opportunities and deliver consistent performance through 2026 and beyond.”

NMDC Group continues to closely monitor regional developments while maintaining a disciplined and forward-looking approach. Supported by a strong backlog, diversified pipeline and solid financial position, the Group remains well-positioned to sustain performance and capitalise on future opportunities.

Article Source: Emirates News Agency (WAM)

data.title: Austria's Erste Group Q1 profit rises 18% on Poland deal
data.description:
Overview

  • Austria banking group's Q1 net profit rose 18% yr/yr, driven by Erste Bank Polska consolidation

  • Q1 net interest income and fee income rose sharply on loan growth and Poland contribution

  • CET1 ratio fell to 14.5% after Poland acquisition, cost/income ratio improved to 45%

Outlook

  • Erste Group targets 2026 net profit above EUR 4 bln, adjusted for extraordinary items

  • Company expects 2026 net interest income above EUR 11 bln and fee income around EUR 4 bln

  • Cost/income ratio projected to improve to about 45% in 2026; risk costs seen at 25-30 bps

Result Drivers

  • POLAND CONSOLIDATION - First-time consolidation of Erste Bank Polska drove large increases in net interest income, fee income, and operating expenses

  • LOAN GROWTH - Loan growth in Slovakia, the Czech Republic, Hungary, and Poland contributed to higher net interest income

  • HIGHER IMPAIRMENT CHARGES - Impairment result rose due to provisions for the Polish loan portfolio required under IFRS 9

Company press release:

data.title: Societe Generale Announces Advance In Q1 Bottom Line
data.description:
PARIS (dpa-AFX) - Societe Generale (SCGLY.PK) announced earnings for its first quarter that Increased from the same period last year

The company's earnings totaled EUR1.696 billion. This compares with EUR1.608 billion last year.

The company's revenue for the period rose 0.3% to EUR7.106 billion from EUR7.083 billion last year.

Societe Generale earnings at glance (GAAP):

-Earnings: EUR1.696 Bln. vs. EUR1.608 Bln. last year.

-Revenue: EUR7.106 Bln vs. EUR7.083 Bln last year.

Slawomir Krupa, CEO of Societe Generale, said: 'This quarter, we continued delivering a strong financial performance. Solid revenue momentum, structural reduction in costs, and ongoing improvement in our cost-to-income ratio led to a 2026 first quarter profitability level well above our full-year target.'

data.title: Volkswagen reports profit decline in first quarter
data.description:
Volkswagen on Thursday reported a 14% decline in operating profit at the start of the year as it faces tariff pressures, geopolitical uncertainty and stiff competition from Chinese brands.

The German auto group, which includes subsidiaries Porsche and Audi, reported first-quarter operating profit of 2.5 billion euros ($2.9 billion).

Analysts had expected profit to remain largely flat at 2.9 billion euros, according to a poll conducted by Visible Alpha.

($1 = 0.8576 euros)

data.title: TE: Record backlog and resilient cash flow despite revenue dip and Middle East challenges
data.description:
Revenue and EBITDA declined YoY due to Middle East disruptions and FX headwinds, but backlog hit a record €20.2bn on strong order intake. Free cash flow conversion remained robust, and guidance for 2026 is conditional on geopolitical normalization.

Original document: Technip Energies NV [TE] Slides Release — Apr. 30 2026
